People dislike true religion because they love things and appearances that allow them to indulge their desires instead of controlling them. It is amazing that a holy God has good-will toward those whose worldly minds are against Him! This shows God’s gracious dealings with the fallen race of mankind, who had turned away from him. This is the covenant of grace He is willing to enter into with them; they must be to Him a people, and He will be to them a God. They must accept the punishment for their sin and not return to foolishness. And it is certain that our troubles are beneficial to us when we are kept from being overcome by the temptations of difficult times.

Here is the application of the parable to Israel. They must long sit like a widow, stripped of all joys and honors; but they will eventually be received again. Those who seek the Lord so as to find Him must turn to Christ and become His willing people. We are to fear the Lord and His greatness, but also the Lord and His goodness; not only His majesty, but His mercy. Even Jewish writers apply this passage to the promised Messiah; it certainly foretold their future conversion to Christ, for which they are kept a separate people. Though the first fear of God arises from seeing His holy majesty and righteous vengeance, the experience of mercy and grace through Jesus Christ will lead the heart to reverence such a kind and glorious Friend and Father, and to fear offending Him.
